note: Pins the exact mock requests/responses the protected tests exercise. Shapes and status codes come from the official docs; concrete handles and fixture values are deterministic mock pins.

submit.omitted_fields_rule: unset optional fields must be absent from the JSON body, not null

polling.path: exactly the statementStatusUrl returned by the server, query string included
polling.wait_rule: every wait goes through the injected wait(ms, signal); never setTimeout directly
polling.deadline_rule: the deadline (injected now() vs deadlineMs) is checked ONLY before scheduling each wait — a poll owed after a completed wait is still issued; the abort signal is checked before each wait AND again when the wait returns (an abort mid-wait means no further live poll); a poll that answers 200 always wins — never cancel after seeing a terminal success

query.requestId: a FRESH UUID, distinct from the submission's

cancel.verify_rule: after a 200 cancel, issue one status GET; the mock answers 422 with the 000604/57014 identity and THAT body is the terminal state the controller reports

success_200.data_rule: rows are arrays of strings; SQL NULL is JSON null

responses.token_rule: the bearer token must never appear in any error text
